name: Hayes, Anthony

Multiple distinct NYC 'Anthony Hayes' profiles exist. Identity not confirmed, so no bio-derived affiliations are recorded.

name: Varghese, Lesley
resolved_employer: Attorney, self-employed (Austin); most recently President & Chief Legal Officer, The SAFE Alliance; former Chief of Staff to Austin Mayor Steve Adler
evidence: Lesley Mary Varghese is a Texas-licensed Austin attorney (State Bar of Texas member; attorney listing at 919 Congress Ave). She served as Chief of Staff to Austin Mayor Steve Adler from Jan 2015 to Jan 2023, then became President & Chief Legal Officer of The SAFE Alliance effective Jan 1, 2023 (Austin Monitor). She is no longer listed on SAFE Alliance's current staff leadership page, consistent with the filing's 'Attorney / Self' self-description. Rare name, 78703 central-Austin zip, attorney occupation, and 42 all-Democratic federal donations corroborate the same person.

affiliations.snippet: Varghese is 'moving to the SAFE Alliance to serve as its new president and chief legal officer, effective Jan. 1.' SAFE serves survivors of child abuse, sexual assault, trafficking and domestic violence.

affiliations.org: Office of Austin Mayor Steve Adler
affiliations.snippet: Article identifies Lesley Varghese as chief of staff for Mayor Steve Adler, departing amid the City Hall transition as the mayor and three council members exited.
Profile lists Varghese as Executive Director & General Counsel at the Asian American Resource Center, Inc., and Affiliate Professor at UT Austin 2012-2014.

name: Moore, Katie

evidence: Jonathan Friedmann is a licensed Austin real estate agent with Friedmann Parks Realty, whose business address (1310 Bentwood Rd, Austin, TX 78722) is an exact match to the donor's filing zip; 16 years in the business, working Austin/Lakeway/Dripping Springs with condo and single-family transactions. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration and no federal PAC record surfaced under this name; a separate 'Jonathan Friedman' on the Northwest Hills (NWACA) board is a different person and was excluded.

name: Mostyn, Amber
resolved_employer: Mostyn Law Firm (co-founder and president; plaintiff-side personal injury / wrongful death trial attorney)
evidence: Amber Mostyn is the co-founder and president of Houston's Mostyn Law, a plaintiff-side personal injury firm; UT Law J.D. 1997. Occupation, employer and Houston 77027 location all match the filing. She is one of Texas's largest Democratic donors — she and her late husband Steve Mostyn gave $5.2 million to federal super PACs in the 2011-2012 cycle alone — consistent with the 366 federal donations on this record.

affiliations.snippet: Center for Public Integrity donor profile lists $1.05 million to House Majority PAC (pro-Democratic) from Amber, part of $5.2 million in Mostyn super PAC giving including $3 million to Priorities USA Action.
$200,000 to Planned Parenthood (pro-Democratic, from Amber) listed among the couple's super PAC contributions.
"Amber Mostyn also sits on the board of Annie's List, a group that supports women Democratic candidates in Texas." Her firm bio adds she served most recently as Chair of Annie's List.
InfluenceWatch lists Mostyn as an advisory board member of Battleground Texas and a founding member of the Texas Future Project.
Listed as a member of the Democracy Alliance, a left-leaning donor collective, and active in efforts to shift Texas from Republican to Democratic control.
Firm bio: "She has held leadership positions in numerous organizations, including the Texas Trial Lawyers Association, Texas Democratic Party, Battleground Texas."
Co-founder of Mostyn Law, a Houston personal injury and wrongful death firm; assumed leadership after her husband Steve Mostyn's death in 2017.

evidence: Clint N. Dawson is a UT Austin professor (Cockrell Family Regents Chair in Engineering #2), department chair in Aerospace Engineering and Engineering Mechanics, and director of the Computational Hydraulics Group at the Oden Institute; his coastal storm-surge modeling work won the 2024 UT President's Research Impact Award. Corroborated by a prior FEC record listing 'Clint Dawson, The University of Texas at Austin, Professor' giving $1,000 to Obama Victory Fund 2012 from an Austin address — occupation, employer and city all match the filing (zip differs, consistent with an in-city move since 2011).

name: Yeager, Jennifer
resolved_employer: Modern Family Law (Senior Attorney, Austin office)
evidence: Jennifer Rhea Yeager, Senior Attorney at Modern Family Law's Austin office; UT Austin B.A. 2002, South Texas College of Law Houston J.D. 2011, Texas bar 2011. Career: criminal law, then an Austin nonprofit serving crime victims, then prosecutor handling family violence and child abuse; founded her own juvenile/criminal-defense firm in 2024 and joined Modern Family Law in January 2026. Name + Austin + 'Attorney' + 'Modern Family Law' all match the filing, corroborated by State Bar of Texas listing. ZIP DISCREPANCY FLAGGED: filing ZIP 79723 is Coahoma/Big Spring in West Texas (Howard County), inconsistent with the 'Austin, TX' location string and with her documented Austin practice; her documented bar ties are Travis, Kerr, and Montgomery counties, so 79723 is most likely a filing data-entry error rather than a second person. FEC record-level retrieval was blocked (api.open.fec.gov DEMO_KEY HTTP 429, OpenSecrets HTTP 403); no ideological-PAC ties surfaced in web search.

affiliations.snippet: Her community involvement includes founding membership in the Travis County Elder Abuse Task Force.

Past presidency of the Kerr County Bar Association (2013-2014); active member of the Montgomery County and Kerr County Bar Associations, Texas Criminal Defense Lawyers Association, and Austin Criminal Defense Lawyers Association.

name: Weiss, Robert
resolved_employer: Self-employed attorney (per filing; not independently verified)
evidence: Could not confirm this specific individual. 'Robert Weiss' is a common name among Chicago attorneys and multiple distinct people surfaced — Robert M. Weiss (Barnes & Thornburg), Robert C. Weiss (VRM Law), Robert B. Weiss (business litigation), John Robert Weiss (Duane Morris). None is a self-employed solo practitioner and none could be tied to ZIP 60614 (Lincoln Park). The donor reports zero FEC donations, so there is no federal donation pattern available to disambiguate. Industry 'Legal' is a categorize-by-employer fallback resting solely on the self-reported occupation 'Lawyer' / employer 'Self-employed' in the filing itself. TX lobbyist registry searched, no match (donor is Illinois-based). Bio search run; no bio page identifiable as this person was located.

name: Garrison, Jessica
resolved_employer: Linder Operating — Director (occupation/employer fields appear swapped in the filing)
evidence: Field-swap confirmed as the likely reading: 'Linder Operating' is the employer and 'Director' the title. 'Linder Operating' most plausibly maps to Linder, John Operating Co., LLC — a confirmed Texas oil & gas operator (Railroad Commission operator #501130, Gladewater, TX), running 90+ leases across East Texas. HOWEVER, the person-to-company link is NOT adequately sourced and the oil_gas category is deliberately NOT applied. A search-result snippet described a 'Jessica Garrison, Senior Financial Operations Analyst at John Linder Operating, Longview, TX', but no verifiable profile page could be retrieved, the same search conflated it with an unrelated Jessica Garrison (an LA Times reporter), and both the city (Longview vs. Austin 78757) and the title (Senior Financial Operations Analyst vs. Director) conflict with the filing. The 'Energy / Environment' classification is a low-confidence employer fallback resting on the donor's self-reported employer string plus the RRC operator-name match — it should not be treated as a verified oil & gas affiliation. FEC record-level retrieval blocked (HTTP 429/403); pipeline fields show 4 donations at partisan lean 1.0, no energy-industry PAC ties surfaced. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration.
